ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-29-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PCN-13-197174 Conservatorship of Frances L. Fox and PTR-13-297196 The Chester I. Fox and Frances L. Fox 1996 Revocable Trust.
Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Thomas Lucas. Helene Fox (Daughter of Conservatee) in Propria Persona.

Mr. Siracusa explained that the Conservatee was originally in a very small board and care home, that his Client did not feel the Conservatee was receiving proper care, that the Conservatee's condition was declining, that she had a fall and that she was moved to Buena Vista Manor. Mr. Siracusa also explained that she suffered another fall, that she needed a skilled nursing facility, that she was then moved to Hayes Convalescent, that his Client hired a private attendant and that the Conservatee is continuing to decline. In addition, Mr. Siracusa stated that there are very few Medi Cal facilities, that he does not have the net result of the liquidated securities, that they are really trying to push on reducing the attendant care, that they have another care conference this month, that he believes all of the overdraft fees were reimbursed and provided a modified order with a $100.00 reduction for the insufficient fund fees. Ms. Fox expressed her concerns about her Mother's funds being depleted, the increase in care costs, her mother being isolated from her and expressed her wishes to have her Mother moved to Hawaii. The Court asked Ms. Fox to put all of her concerns in writing. Mr. Siracusa stated that the facility was told to speak with Ms. Fox. The accounting petitions were taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
